ssl_pas_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — your [town] [business type] site is flagged 'Not Secure'
subject_examples: Mark — your Knaresborough plumbing site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Julie — your Skipton B&B website is flagged 'Not Secure'
David — your Ripon estate agency site isn't secure
Helen — your Boroughbridge joinery site is flagged 'Not Secure'
Sarah — your Settle builder website is flagged 'Not Secure'
ssl_pas_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

I was looking up [trade] in [town] this morning and noticed [specific URL] comes up with a 'Not Secure' warning in Chrome.

For visitors searching for a [trade] in [town], that warning appears before they've even read your page — it's the first thing they see. A lot will click away immediately.

The fix is quick and doesn't touch your existing site or content. Would it be worth a quick chat?

[Your name]
personalisation_notes: Use Chrome's exact wording: 'Not Secure' — not 'unsecured'
Include the specific URL — shows you actually looked
Town name in opening line — local credibility
One pain point only — the SSL warning. Nothing else.
No question marks in subject line

mobile_pas_day0.condition: SSL clean AND mobile clearly broken — must have personally tested
mobile_pas_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — your [town] [trade] site doesn't work on mobile
subject_examples: Mark — your Knaresborough plumber site is hard to use on a phone
Julie — your Skipton salon website doesn't work well on mobile
mobile_pas_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

I looked up [business name] on my phone this morning and the website was difficult to use — [specific issue: text too small / buttons too close together / layout broken / form not usable].

Most people in [town] searching for a [trade] are on their phone. If they can't read or use your site easily, they'll go to the next result.

This is usually a straightforward fix. Worth a look?

[Your name]
personalisation_notes: Must describe the SPECIFIC mobile failure you observed — not generic 'mobile issues'
Only use this signal if SSL is clean (HTTPS) — otherwise use SSL-PAS
Mention that you tested on your phone — shows credibility

no_website_bab_day0.condition: 4★+ AND 20+ Google reviews with confirmed owner name. Rural bar.
no_website_bab_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — [town] [trade]: you're on Google Maps but not Google search
subject_examples: Mark — your Knaresborough builder business isn't showing in Google search
Julie — your Skipton B&B has great reviews but no website
no_website_bab_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

I came across [business name] on Google Maps — [X] reviews, [rating] stars, impressive for a [trade] in [town].

The problem is that when someone searches for '[trade] [town]' on Google, [business name] doesn't come up. The Maps listing is there, but there's no website for Google to rank.

A simple one-page site would change that — people searching for a [trade] in [town] would find you instead of your competitors.

Would that be useful?

[Your name]
personalisation_notes: Lead with the strength (good reviews) BEFORE the gap
Quote the exact review count and star rating — shows you checked
Reference the specific search query they're missing: '[trade] [town]'
BAB: Before (good Maps presence) → After (ranking in search) → Bridge (simple site)

aida_day0.condition: 4.5★+ AND 100+ reviews ONLY. Verifiable keyword gap confirmed.
aida_day0.subject_primary: [First name] — [business name] should be ranking for '[trade] [town]'
aida_day0.body:
Hi [First name],

[Business name] has [X] reviews and [rating] stars — that's one of the strongest reputations for a [trade] in [town].

I checked Google this morning and you're not appearing in the top results for '[trade] [town]'. Someone with fewer reviews and a weaker reputation is ranking ahead of you.

There's a specific reason for that — and it's fixable. Worth a quick conversation?

[Your name]
personalisation_notes: AIDA only for strongest review profiles (4.5★+ AND 100+ reviews)
Verifiable keyword gap required — must have checked the actual search
Name the competitor category (not specific names) to add credibility

ssl_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Just following up on my note from Tuesday — the 'Not Secure' warning is still showing on [URL].

If the timing isn't right at the moment, no problem — happy to revisit when it suits. Or if you have any questions, just reply here.

[Your name]

mobile_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Following up from earlier this week — the mobile layout on [URL] is still showing the issue I mentioned.

Happy to walk through what the fix would look like if that's useful. Or let me know if the timing doesn't work.

[Your name]

no_website_bump.body:
Hi [First name],

Just a quick follow-up from Tuesday — [business name] still isn't appearing in Google search for '[trade] [town]', while some competitors with fewer reviews are showing up.

If it's something you'd like to explore, I'm happy to explain the options. No pressure if the timing's off.

[Your name]

batch_6_day7_templates.note: Day-7 new angle — STANDALONE new email. New subject. DIFFERENT pain point from Day-0. NOT a thread reply.
batch_6_day7_templates.rule: Day-7 MUST use a genuinely different pain point. A reframe of Day-0 produces 0% response.

ssl_to_ranking_pivot.subject: [First name] — HTTP sites rank lower in Google local search
ssl_to_ranking_pivot.body:
Hi [First name],

I mentioned [URL] shows as 'Not Secure' earlier this week. I wanted to add one thing that's less obvious.

Google's local search algorithm penalises HTTP sites — they rank lower than equivalent HTTPS sites, all else being equal. For '[trade] [town]' searches, that can be the difference between appearing in the top three and not appearing at all.

It's a quick fix that resolves both the warning and the ranking penalty in one go. Happy to explain how.

[Your name]

mobile_to_ssl_pivot.subject: [First name] — also noticed [URL] shows as 'Not Secure'
mobile_to_ssl_pivot.body:
Hi [First name],

I wrote earlier about the mobile layout on [URL]. I also noticed the site is running on HTTP — Chrome shows it as 'Not Secure' to every visitor.

The two issues are separate fixes, but they both affect whether customers stay or leave. Worth sorting both at once if you're going to look at the site anyway.

[Your name]

no_website_to_enquiry_estimate.subject: [First name] — how many [town] [trade] searches are you missing?
no_website_to_enquiry_estimate.body:
Hi [First name],

Following up from earlier this week. I did a quick check — '[trade] [town]' gets searched [X]+ times per month on Google in your area.

Every one of those searches is a potential customer who can't find [business name] online. Some of them will have gone to a competitor with a website.

A one-page site is the minimum to start appearing. Happy to share what that would cost and look like.

[Your name]

batch_6_day14_templates.note: Day-14 breakup — STANDALONE new email. 3 lines max. No pressure. Door open. Final touchpoint.
batch_6_day14_templates.rule: 3 lines maximum. No pressure language. No question marks. Leave door open. Final touchpoint — 4-touchpoint limit enforced.

ssl_breakup.subject: [First name] — leaving this with you
ssl_breakup.body:

I'll leave this with you — the 'Not Secure' warning on [URL] is still there whenever you're ready to sort it.

Feel free to get in touch if the timing ever works.

[Your name]

mobile_breakup.body:
Hi [First name],

I'll leave this with you — the mobile layout issue on [URL] is still there whenever you want to look at it.

Feel free to get back in touch if things change.

[Your name]

no_website_breakup.subject: [First name] — leaving this with you
no_website_breakup.body:

I'll leave this one with you — the option to get [business name] ranking in Google search is there whenever the timing works.

Feel free to get in touch when it suits.

[Your name]
